title: hieracium murorum subsp. subholophyllum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. subholophyllum, also known as Hill hawkweed, is a rare species of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Traditionally, Hill hawkweed has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve digestive issues such as bloating and indigestion. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are believed to have health benefits including reducing inflammation and improving circulation. However, further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Hill hawkweed for medicinal purposes, and it should be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
